summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Relative Dating (Superposition, Cross-Cutting)
summary_sections.content_html:
Relative dating determines the order of geological events without assigning specific ages.

  • Law of Superposition: in undisturbed sedimentary layers, the oldest rocks are at the bottom, youngest at the top.
  • Principle of Original Horizontality: sedimentary layers are originally deposited horizontally; tilted layers indicate later deformation.
  • Principle of Cross-Cutting Relationships: a fault or intrusion is younger than the rock it cuts through.
  • Principle of Inclusion: fragments of rock included within another rock are older than the surrounding rock.
  • Unconformities: gaps in the rock record showing missing time (angular unconformity, disconformity, nonconformity).
